mapping.a_element: Hopf algebra of rooted trees ${\cal H}_R$ with a single primitive divergence
mapping.b_element: Kreimer's Hopf algebra on the symmetric algebra of rooted trees

mapping.a_element: coproduct iterations used to compute dimensions of subspaces
mapping.b_element: coproduct defined on Kreimer's Hopf algebra

mapping.a_element: bigrading by number of nodes $n$ and degree of primitivity $k$
mapping.b_element: grading by count of non-root vertices
Grossman-Larson Hopf algebra (noncommutative multiplication on rooted trees)
extension of growth operator to Kreimer's Hopf algebra

brief_justification: Both papers study Hopf algebras of rooted trees with a focus on combinatorial structures and operators (e.g., coproducts, growth/pruning operators, and gradings). The core machinery—Hopf algebraic constructions, grading by tree size, and operators that modify tree structure—maps directly, with $B_+$ in A playing the role of the growth operator in B, and the bigrading in A refining the grading in B.
